overview.school: School of Computing and Creative Technologies
overview.duration: September start - one year full-time; two years part-time; January start - one year full-time.
overview.delivery: Full-time; part-time; starts September and January
accreditations_and_partnerships: BCS, The Chartered Institute for IT
overview.summary: Work with us full or part time on our industry-focused data science master's, developing the skills you need to design and implement data science projects.

why_study_data_science: Data science brings together data management, analytics and artificial intelligence.
Data scientists work with organisations to draw competitive or efficiency-related insights from their data.
The field is expected to make up at least a quarter of all digital jobs.
It has been highlighted as a major skills gap in the government's recent industrial strategy.
why_uwe_bristol: The master's is designed for people looking to specialise or upskill in data science.
It equips students to apply data science techniques in their current role and organisation, or to progress into new opportunities.
Students learn the full data science pipeline: organisational requirements, ethical conduct, research study design, data engineering, gathering, transforming and cleaning small and large-scale data.
Students gain skills and tools to design and implement data science projects and programmes to solve real business and societal issues.
Students undertake exploratory analysis using statistics, machine learning and predictive modelling.
Students learn to present and communicate results to stakeholders and implement production workflows and solutions.
Students work on live data science projects using data and issues from their own company, where applicable, and industrial partners.
where_can_it_take_me: The course was developed as part of the Institute of Coding's University Learners employability initiative.
It is designed to equip data scientists with skills needed by industry.
Career routes include data scientist, business analyst, data engineer and chief data officer.

entry_requirements: Honours degree at 2:2 or equivalent in a relevant subject.
Experience with quantitative methods and/or coding is highly recommended.

Relevant subjects include Computer Science, IT or other computing subjects, Maths, Statistics, Engineering, quantitative subjects such as Physics and Chemistry, Business, Marketing, Economics, Psychology and Social Sciences.
Applicants who do not meet the grade requirements may be considered individually if they have at least 12 months relevant professional experience and/or equivalent qualifications.
entry.personal_statement: Applicants are required to complete a personal statement and answer the questions on the postgraduate application form within the personal statement tabs, keeping to the advised word count.
english_language_requirement: International and EU applicants need a minimum IELTS Academic score of 6.5 overall with 5.5 in each component, or an approved equivalent.
UWE accepts a large number of UK and international qualifications in place of IELTS.
how_to_apply: Postgraduate applications information is available from UWE.
International applications and key international deadline dates are available from UWE.

structure.content_note: The optional modules listed are those most likely to be available, but they may be subject to change.
core_modules: Process and Practice in Data Science
structure.part_time_note: The listed structure is for full-time students only. Part-time students study the same modules but the delivery pattern is different.
structure.course_change_note: The University continually enhances the course in response to feedback and stakeholder input. Approved changes will be communicated to students.

learning_and_teaching: The course is taught through a mix of context, theory and hands-on practice.
Both individual and group learning activities are included.
Students study the role of a data scientist and areas such as ethical practice and data for sustainable development.
Students cover research methods, data gathering, exploratory data analysis, and programming principles including R, Python and HTML/Javascript.
Students learn statistical inference, modelling and analysis, machine learning and predictive analytics.
Students learn to store, process and analyse big data.
Students build skills in evidence-based communication, argumentation and data visualisation.
Students implement data science projects end to end using real data for business, health and sustainability problems.
Students gain exposure to current data science methods and tools.
Students take part in a substantial interdisciplinary group project.
Extracurricular opportunities include team competitions, data hackathons and paid projects for external clients through The Foundry.
Mentoring is available for self-organised student teams in data science competitions and hackathons.

study_time.full_time: Over one year: 8 hours a week of teaching and related activities, and 16 hours a week on self-directed study.
study_time.part_time: Over two years: 4 hours a week of teaching and related activities, and 8 hours a week on self-directed study.

assessment: Number of exams depends on optional module choices

supplementary_fee_information: Funding and studentship opportunities are listed on UWE's Scholarships and bursaries page.
More information is available on funding and scholarships.
Postgraduate funding information is available from UWE.

features.professional_accreditation: The course is accredited by BCS, The Chartered Institute for IT.
study_facilities: Frenchay Campus PC labs for module and self-study.
Access to virtual machines and cloud-based environments to build big data solution experience.
24-hour access to the UWE Bristol library.
Access to specialist journals and publications through the online portal.
Dedicated graduate student space on the main campus with teaching rooms and informal areas.
Student adviser providing pastoral support and general advice.
Access to UWE's enterprise zone, Future Space.
Personalised virtual tour of Computing facilities.

careers.careers_further_study: Knowledge of current data science methods and tools should put graduates in a strong position to secure work as a data scientist, business analyst, data engineer or to develop a career path toward chief data officer.

life.title: Postgraduate support
life.description: Support includes access to facilities, study tools and career consultants, plus practical help with funding and childcare.

life.title: Bristol
life.description: A city for student living with qualities that may make students want to stay.

life.title: Sports, societies and activities
life.description: There is more to the student experience than study, with opportunities to try new things.

life.description: UWE provides support in the way students need it.
